context_name: WGQS-350-72-2262 - Gender, Race, Culture, Science & Technology

assignment.description:
Purpose

The weekly Discussion Forum allows students to assess the week's material, share some of what they learned in the weekly activity, and communicate and collaborate in this process with others in the class through the interactive discussion.

Tasks

    1. After reading, watching, and listening to the required materials and completing the reflection activity, post to this forum. To receive credit, your post must include the following:
      1. What are the two most important takeaways from this week's required material? (That is, what are the most important points from the week's required material for our work in WGQS/ES 350 class?)
      2. Share which praxis activity you chose and something you learned from the Praxis Activity
      3. Your post must include at least one direct quote and cite at least one source from the week's required materials.
        • When you quote from course materials, please include parenthetical citations (for instance, with the author’s last name and page number of the citation). 
        • Be sure to explain why you think the quote was important or connect it to your takeaways. 
      4. Reply to a post that has no more than one other existing reply (until all posts have at least one reply). 
        • First, identify something that they stated or cited that you thought was insightful and explain why OR identify something in particular that you thought was related to your own analysis and explain why.
        • Second, building on their post, what is something that might be further explored? How is it related to their post? 

How to Post

To begin your initial post, use the Reply button at the bottom of this page. Create a descriptive Subject line related to your post content (e.g., something like: "Assimilate in STEM fields (or perish)"), and input your post text into the "Message" field. 

You may choose to complete this in a word-processing program first so you can save your work. Then copy and paste into Canvas after it is edited. Use the formatting toolbar in Canvas as desired.

To create your replies: Click on the Discussion thread (indicated by the Subject line) for the reading response post to which you would like to reply. In the bottom right corner of the Message of the post, you will see a link for "Reply" (next to a link that reads "Permalink"). Click on "Reply," and you will get a new "Message" box in which you can input your reply (feel free to use the formatting toolbar if you wish).

Time Frame

To complete this discussion, your initial posting is due by Friday, Feb 13 (end of the day). The response is due by Saturday, Feb 14 (end of the day). (You are always welcome and able to complete all the coursework for the week by Friday if you choose.)

Criteria for Success

Students who have completed ALL FOUR TASKS (in a post and response) will receive credit and students who have not completed ALL FOUR TASKS will not receive credit.
assignment.name: Week 6 Discussion Forum (Praxis Activity Week)

discussion_topic.title: Week 6 Discussion Forum (Praxis Activity Week)

assignment.description:
GWR Portfolio Criteria Checklist

Once you have prepared all three documents for the GWR Portfolio, you must complete the GWR Portfolio Checklist using your Cal Poly email address. The checklist is a Microsoft Form designed to:

  • confirm that your portfolio materials meet the basic criteria, and
  • collect the required academic integrity attestation for an asynchronous writing course.

There is no file upload on this assignment page. Instead, reviewers will mark this assignment as complete only after you have completed the checklist Form.

⚠️ Important: You cannot earn credit for the GWR Portfolio without completing the checklist. The checklist must be submitted before you upload your final portfolio documents.
assignment.name: Completing the Criteria Checklist

assignment.description:
After completing all steps in the UNIV 401: GWR Portfolio process, including completing the GWR Portfolio Checklist, you must upload all three final portfolio documents together in response to this assignment.

Important: Submitting fewer than three documents will result in an incomplete submission.

Note: Late submissions will be accepted only with prior approval for an extenuating circumstance.

File Naming Instructions

Before uploading, save and name each Portfolio file exactly as shown below:

  • LastName_FirstInitial_Intro
  • LastName_FirstInitial_Paper1
  • LastName_FirstInitial_Paper2

Example:

  • Doe_J_Intro
  • Doe_J_Paper1
  • Doe_J_Paper2

Important: Do not add extra text, spaces, or numbering to the file names.

Required Document Heading

Each of your three documents must include the following information in the top left corner of page 1:

  • Your name
  • Subject and course number
  • Instructor name
  • Date the paper was completed (include the year)
  • Paper number and assignment title

Example Heading

Jane Doe

HIST 345

Dr. Smith

September 22, 2023

Paper #1: An Analysis of the Battle of Gettysburg

Note: You do not need to change your citation style (APA, MLA, etc.).
Simply add the required heading to page 1 of Paper #1, Paper #2, and the Portfolio Introduction using the format shown above.
assignment.name: Uploading the GWR Portfolio

context_name: WGQS-350-72-2262 - Gender, Race, Culture, Science & Technology

assignment.description:
Week 6 Praxis Activity 

Purpose

This activity allows students to explore weekly student learning objectives through their own further research and application. 

Knowledge

This activity further explores the role of science in maintaining and enforcing the sex binary and the impact of this delineation.

Tasks

  1. Read, watch, and listen to the week's required materials.
  2. Choose one of the praxis activities described below.
  3. Submit Documentation of your research: Take a screen shot of an article you read, print an interview transcript (without identifying information), or create other relevant documentation to demonstrate your work in the praxis activity. 
  4. Submit a Write-Up: Follow the required structure/format. Give an Introduction that explains Praxis Activity you chose and why, give a Review of Research that lists each of the sources reviewed and a one- to two-sentence summary (like an annotated bibliography or mini literature review), and finally analytically engage, Answering all the questions from the prompt (below). 

Week 6 Praxis Activities (choose one)

1. Sex testing in the Olympics

We have read about the history of sex testing in the Olympics (Fausto-Sterling). On September 25 2019, the Olympic Channel reported on the IAAF's new DSD (Differences of Sexual Development) regulations. These require a certain maximum level of testosterone in order for a runner to compete in the women's category. Read Goh et al., DSD athletes: What does it mean to be DSD and how gender and sex are the big issues in athletics. Subsequent this, the World Athletics (formerly IAAF) revised the requirements for Olympic athletes. Read Section entitled "Transgender and DSD Regulations" of 23 March 2023 Press Release (pg. 3-4 of PDF).

Listen to/read the following to familiarize yourself with the consequences of these through individual athletes' cases:

  • Watch The Stream (2014) "No Games for Women With Too Much Testosterone" (Focusing on DSD regulations prior to and during Dutee Chand's appeal)
  • Listen to final episode of NPR's Tested: Lumpers and Splitters (2024) (Highlighting athletes' options given the 2023 regulations)
  • Find one other article (popular press or scholarly) addressing sex testing (or verification of belonging in the "women" category for competition) from 2024 or later. (For instance, there are cases from Summer Olympics of 2024.)
  • Answer the following questions: 
    • What was one of the The Stream panelists' objection to or argument in support of the IAAF/World Athletics former policy on on limiting testosterone for women competing in the Olympics? Can you identify a concept being utilized from our Week 2 readings in this position?
    • From reporting in Tested, what choices do athletes have after the 2023 changes in DSD Regulations? 
    • Does the article you found have a different perspective than the panelist's you have chosen to spotlight? What insights does it add to the current situation?

2. The so-called "Fairness in Women's Sports Act" 

In 2020, the Idaho legislature passed the Idaho “Fairness in Women’s Sports Act.” Since then many other states have signed into similar legislation. Even the U.S. House of Representatives passed a bill "H.R. 734 - Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act of 2023" in March 2023, but the U.S. Senate tabled the bill. The Biden administration created a rule to nullify such laws by including transgendered people as a protected class, protected by Title IX but it was blocked by a Supreme Court ruling in August 2024. In the case of Idaho, a court blocked the implementation of the "Fairness in Women's Sports Act" while the law goes through the appeal process.

The bill bars transgender girls and women from participating in women’s school and college sports. Lindsay Hecox appealed the law in Idaho and for the appeal processes various amicus briefs, or "friend of the court" briefs, have been filed. Many groups have filed briefs against the law (including The American Medical Association and the American Academy of Pediatrics). In addition, various medical professionals contributed to an amicus brief in defense of the original “Fairness in Women’s Sports Act.” 

  • Read through this Brief of Amici Curiae Medical Professionals Supporting Intervenors-Appellants and Urging Reversal (especially Section I and Section III)
    • Evaluate it for its use of biological determinism, reductionism, and the concept of gender and sex employed.  How do the "friends of the court" who have filed this brief depend on these and what is the limitation of this?
  • Find at least 2 newspaper articles that report on Fairness in Women's Sports Act (in Idaho, Florida, Arkansas, and in other states pushing for similar legislation).
    • How do these reports frame the legislation?
    • Do these reports name the problem of biological determinism and reductionism? What analytical lenses do reporters use? 
    • What recommendations do you have for news organizations and reporters?

Criteria for Success

After completing the praxis activity, submit Documentation and Write-Up as described above.

The rubric for grading is attached to the assignment. (10 points).
assignment.name: Week 6 Praxis Activity Documentation and Write-Up

rubric.description: Provided Documentation of the Praxis Activity Work

ratings.long_description: Provide documentation, evidencing the work undertaken. (screenshot of article read, transcript of interview, screenshot of data analyzed, etc.)
No documentation provided.
Explain which Praxis Activity was chosen and why.
Do not explain which Praxis Activity was chosen and why.
List all the sources reviewed, those researched in addition to what was provided.
Only listed all sources OR Only provided explanation for some (not all required per the prompt)
No mention of sources researched and read.

rubric.description: Answered the Questions in the Prompt

ratings.long_description: Answered all the questions in the prompt, demonstrating understanding and analytical engagement with the praxis activity materials.
Answering some of the questions of the prompt, demonstrating analytical engagement with the praxis activity materials.
Some or all of the questions answered without demonstrating analytical engagement with the praxis activity materials.
No questions from the prompt answered.

assignment.description:
Week 6 Homework:

  • Make sure you review the Homework Submission Protocol section of the syllabus before turning in your homework.
  • Upload a single R script file with the code for all of Week 6 homework. But before you upload it, change the extension to .txt so that the grader can view the file within the Canvas grading environment.
  • Name your file something akin to HW06MurphyJ.txt to indicate the homework number and your name.
  • Your file should have a header indicating your name and perhaps a partner's. See syllabus.
  • I hope you found this homework to be good practice for this week's topics!
